perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement the following definitions shall apply: (a) Authorized Locations means the locations listed in Schedule "C."
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement the following definitions shall apply: (a) Authorized Locations means all locations where Sparkling Spring Water Group Limited operates, irrespective of whether they are listed in Schedule "C."
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Authorized Locations' is modified to include all locations operated by Sparkling, overriding the specific list in Schedule C. This creates a contradiction because other sections of the contract rely on Schedule C to define authorized locations, especially regarding software copies and fees. This change introduces an in-text contradiction, because Schedule C could include new location or can exclude them
perturbation.contradicted_text: Garman hereby grants to Sparkling, for the use of Sparkling and affiliated companies of Sparkling operating at Authorized Locations, a non-transferable and non-exclusive licence to use: (i) the Software, safely in executable object code format, at each AS400 at each of the Authorized Locations; and (ii) the Documentation. Sparkling's right, if any, to use the Software and Documentation at locations other than the Authorized Locations is subject to the payment of additional fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Authorized Locations' directly contradicts the section that relies on this definition for software licensing and fee calculation. The original text limits software use to specific locations in Schedule C, while the changed text expands it to all Sparkling locations, rendering Schedule C meaningless for determining authorized use and creating an ambiguity about which locations require additional fees. The contradiction is that the contradicted text references the fees associated with usage of software at locations that are not 'Authorized Locations' but this new definition of 'Authorized Location' creates a contradiction because ALL Sparkling Locations are 'Authorized Locations' as per the Changed Text. This introduces an in-text contradiction, because Schedule C could include new locations or can exclude them.

perturbation.original_text: Should the Software not operate substantially in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications in all material respects, Garman will use its best efforts to repair the Software under the support agreement at no additional charge to Sparkling.
perturbation.changed_text: Should the Software not operate in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications, Garman may choose to repair the Software under the support agreement at an additional charge to Sparkling based on market rates. Garman will notify sparkling with the new payment fees.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract states that Garman will repair software issues at no additional charge, but the modified text allows Garman to charge Sparkling for repairs, conflicting with the original promise of 'no additional charge.' This creates uncertainty regarding the cost of maintenance and support. This is an in-text contradiction because the original contract specifies a one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of maintenance fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges. This in-text contradiction creates conflict on the fee rates to be charged to Sparkling
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a renewable one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of maintenance fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services: (a) SOFTWARE UPDATE SERVICE As part of the Software Update Service, Garman shall provide to Sparkling as soon as reasonably available: (1) corrections and bug fixes for the Software; (2) all modifications, refinements, and enhancements ("Improvements") of the Software; (3) new releases of the Software; and (4) updated user manuals to support all of the above in a reasonable time frame. (b) TELEPHONE SUPPORT SERVICE Telephone Support Service includes Sparkling having direct telephone access to employees of Garman who have the necessary technical expertise and experience to understand and consider Sparkling's inquiries concerning the failure of Software to operate according to Specifications and to clarify Documentation that is either insufficient or unclear. Such direct telephone access shall be available in accordance with the Telephone Support Schedule. In the event that the Telephone Support Service does provide a resolution to Sparkling (acting reasonably) within 2 business days to permit the Software to meet the Specifications, then Garman shall provide Software Repair Services to address the Software failure. The existing Telephone support numbers are for Garman business hours 1-800-667-6901 or 1-306-242-6322 PAGE 5 and after hours 1-306-717-1999, which is a pager number being monitored directly by qualified Garman staff. (c) SOFTWARE REPAIR SERVICE Should the Software not operate substantially in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications in all material respects, Garman will use its best efforts to repair the Software under the support agreement at no additional charge to Sparkling.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly states that Garman will repair software issues at 'no additional charge.' The changed text allows Garman to charge 'additional' fees based on market rates. This directly contradicts the 'without additional fees or charges' clause in the contradicted text regarding Maintenance Services. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the fee structure for software repairs.

perturbation.original_text: Sparkling may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ninety (90) calendar days written notice to Garman. If Sparkling terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Section 17(b), Sparkling shall cease using the Software at the end of the ninety (90) day notice period, and shall certify to Garman within thirty (30) calendar days of termination that Sparkling has destroyed or has returned to Garman all Software, Documentation and Codes.
perturbation.changed_text: Sparkling may terminate this Agreement at any time with immediate effect by paying Garman a termination fee equivalent to one year of maintenance fees as specified in Schedule A. If Sparkling terminates this Agreement pursuant to this Section 17(b), Sparkling shall cease using the Software immediately upon payment of the termination fee, and shall certify to Garman within ten (10) calendar days of termination that Sparkling has destroyed or has returned to Garman all Software, Documentation and Codes.
perturbation.explanation: The modification alters the termination clause, requiring immediate payment and reducing the certification period, in contradiction to the original 90-day notice period. This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty in the procedure of termination, potentially leading to disputes over compliance and fees. This is an in-text contradiction because the first line of 17(a) states This Agreement and the license granted under this Agreement shall remain in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule and the Agreement is not otherwise terminated in accordance with this Section. This implies the continuation and is intext contradicted
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement and the license granted under this Agreement shall remain in effect perpetually as long as fees are paid by Sparkling in accordance with the Fee Schedule and the Agreement is not otherwise terminated in accordance with this Section.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement stated a perpetual agreement as long as fees were paid (Contradicted Text). The changed text in Section 17(b) introduces immediate termination upon payment of a fee, directly contradicting the condition for perpetual continuation outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'. This creates an in-text contradiction by establishing conflicting conditions for the agreement's termination (Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction), leading to uncertainty about the duration and termination procedures.
